zh.lifeSummary: 當Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr於1270年出生在英國威爾斯時，他的父親Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr60歲，母親Efa verch Llewellyn58歲。他和Gwenillian ferch Cydifor至少有12個兒子和4個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr于1270年出生在英国威尔斯时，他的父亲Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr60岁，母亲Efa verch Llewellyn58岁。他和Gwenillian ferch Cydifor至少有12个儿子和4个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .
English: variant of   Pape   1.

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Cadwgon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Welsh:  see  Cadogan  .

variants.name: Cadogan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.

Welsh (Pembrokeshire and Glamorgan) and Irish (of Welsh origin): from the Old Welsh personal name Catgucaun, Catguocawn, modernized as Cadwgon. It is composed of cad ‘battle’ + gogawn ‘honour, glory’. See also Wogan . The surname is stressed, like its Welsh original, on the penultimate syllable, hence its occasional development to Duggan .
Irish: from Ó Ceadagáin ‘descendant of Ceadagán’.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .
English: variant of   Pape   1.

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Fawr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English fear(r), farre (Old English fearr) ‘steer, ox’, applied as a nickname for a fierce man or a metonymic occupational name for someone who kept a bull.
German: nickname from Middle High German varne, var, with the same meaning as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Cadwgan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: from Old Welsh cad ‘battle’ + gwogawn ‘glory, distinction’, or ‘honour’; the name of several Welsh princes in the early Middle Ages and of two characters mentioned in the Mabinogi.

variants.name: Cadogan
meanings: Anglicized form of   Cadwgan .  It was revived in the 19th century, perhaps in part as a transferred use of the surname.

variants.name: Cadwgawn
meanings: Traditional: from Old Welsh cad ‘battle’ + gwogawn ‘glory, distinction’, or ‘honour’; the name of several Welsh princes in the early Middle Ages and of two characters mentioned in the Mabinogi.

nameMeanings.name: Fychan
meanings: Welsh: A Welsh name of great antiquity, from vyckan, little, small; i.e. small in stature. Compare the surname Bychan and the Armoric Le Bihan.

en.lifeSummary: When Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr was born in 1270, in Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr, was 60 and his mother, Efa verch Llewellyn, was 58. He had at least 12 sons and 4 daughters with Gwenillian ferch Cydifor.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr est né en 1270, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Efa verch Llewellyn, avait 58 ans. Il a eu au moins 12 fils et 4 filles avec Gwenillian ferch Cydifor.

de.lifeSummary: Als Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr 1270 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr 60 und seine Mutter Efa verch Llewellyn 58. Er hatte mit Gwenillian ferch Cydifor mindestens 12 Söhne und 4 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr è nato il nel 1270 nel Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr, aveva 60 anni e il suo madre, Efa verch Llewellyn, aveva 58 anni. Lui avuto almeno 12 figli e 4 figlie con Gwenillian ferch Cydifor.

ja.lifeSummary: Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawrが1270年にイギリスウェールズで生まれた時，父Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawrは60歳，母Efa verch Llewellynは58歳であった。彼はGwenillian ferch Cydiforとの間に少なくとも12人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr(이)가 1270년 영국 웨일스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr(은)는 60세였고, 그의 어머니 Efa verch Llewellyn(은)는 58세였다. 그는 Gwenillian ferch Cydifor(와)과 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr родился в 1270 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Efa verch Llewellyn, 58. У него было по крайней мере 12 сыновей и 4 дочери от Gwenillian ferch Cydifor.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Cadwgan Fychan ap Cadwgon ap Fawr nació en 1270, en Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Cadwgon ap Gruffydd Fawr, tenía 60 años y su mamá, Efa verch Llewellyn, tenía 58 años. Tuvo por lo menos 12 hijos y 4 hijas con Gwenillian ferch Cydifor.
